goal: 整合 cxs 多源工作流 A1 A2 A3 handoff

summary.title: 整合多源工作流前置调研结果
summary.what: Added canonical handoffs for A1, A2, and A3, replaced pending worker ids with actual thread ids, and moved workflow-state to Wave 2 ready.
summary.why: The orchestrator must reconcile worker results into durable files before launching D1. This prevents D1 from depending on ephemeral chat summaries and records the evidence packets needed for architecture design.
summary.user_goal: 整合 cxs 多源工作流 A1 A2 A3 handoff

decisions.chose: Write compact canonical handoffs instead of pasting full worker transcripts.
decisions.rationale: D1 needs durable evidence and decisions, but the control plane should remain terse.

decisions.chose: Mark A1/A2/A3 verified after direct read_thread checks and handoff reconciliation.
decisions.rationale: Each worker completed read-only work and returned proof/status without source changes.

decisions.chose: Move workflow-state to Wave 2 ready and make D1 the next launch step.
decisions.rationale: The required read-only packets are now available.

review_notes: Docs-only orchestration update; no runtime, CLI, DB, parser, source, or release skill behavior changed.
The update intentionally overlaps earlier proposed workflow-control intents because it updates the same durable registry and handoff directory.
A3 used official web docs and structural-only local sampling; no private transcript content was copied.

architectural_claims: A1 found Codex-only assumptions in root resolution, parser, source inventory, selector/coverage, DB identity, CLI defaults, and release skill text.
A2 recommends source_id as an explicit DB, selector, coverage, and query dimension with Codex as default.
A3 recommends reserving Claude Code as a future adapter while keeping it unpublished in this phase.
security_implications: The Claude risk handoff records structural-only sampling and forbids copying private transcript text, tool output, paths from records, or secrets.
migration_notes: No migration implemented; A2 outlines source-aware identity and migration decisions for D1/I2.
